batch_summary: Deed [119] PDF pulled (LC Vol. D, Pg. 24, Inst# DR-0000D-00024). Right-of-way conveyance from James H. Bounds to Houston & Texas Central Railway Company. Execution date is May 3, 1871 — NOT March 22, 1875 as the county index stated; index date is erroneous. Dual recording: originally Book II pp. 798–799 (filed 5/3/1871); certified copy re-filed Book D p. 24 (6/22/1895). Conveys 200-foot strip over the 'Hall tract' on the Sarah W. McNulty (McAnulty) League plus blanket ROW over all other JH Bounds lands, for $375 coin. Fee simple — no mineral reservation. CRITICAL DISCOVERY: identifies T.J. Hall and wife as a SECOND upstream McAnulty source (deed dated Dec 15, 1865), distinct from the Hare→Bounds source identified by deed [123]. Dec 15, 1865 is now the earliest documented date for any JH Bounds McAnulty League activity. Survey name variant: 'Sarah W. McNulty' (middle initial W., alternate surname spelling). DISPOSED — ROW CONVEYANCE OUT, NO REVERSION. Filename assigns [301] but instrument is [119] — no new deed number needed.

operations.content: 1a. **T.J. Hall → JH Bounds (Dec 15, 1865) — SECOND upstream source identified**: Deed [119] (1871-05-03, JH Bounds → H&TC Railway, ROW) describes the ROW land as "the Hall tract a part of the Sarah W. McNulty league... having been conveyed to me by T.J. Hall and wife by deed dated 15th Dec 1865." This is a SEPARATE upstream McAnulty acquisition from the Hare→Bounds deed — different grantor, different date. Dec 15, 1865 is now the **earliest documented JH Bounds McAnulty League activity**, consistent with the family tree note that JH Bounds "migrated to Texas 1865." Acreage unknown. Recording citation unknown — **action: search LC grantee index under JH Bounds for Hall conveyance, Dec 1865**. ⚠️ Survey name variant: deed [119] uses "Sarah W. McNulty" (middle initial "W." and alternate spelling) — same survey as "Sarah McAnulty" in all other instruments.

operations.find:
#### Deed [119] — J. H. Bounds → Houston & Texas Central Railway Co. (1875-03-22, Limestone)
- **Transaction type**: Right-of-way conveyance — McAnulty League
- **Citation**: LC Vol. D, Pg. 24, Inst# DR-0000D-00024
- **Property**: Sarah McAnulty League (acreage not stated in index)
- **Significance**: Source deed for the H&TC Railroad ROW that runs through the McAnulty League. This ROW is the boundary feature referenced in deeds [22], [05]/[23], [116], and all downstream instruments, and the reason the Manning 3-acre Second Tract is stated as 2 acres net in deed [22] and [116]. Predates all documented McAnulty acquisitions — earliest known JH Bounds McAnulty League transaction.
- **Disposition status**: **DISPOSED — ROW CONVEYANCE OUT.** No reversion clause documented from index. Pull PDF to confirm terms and mineral status. [INDEX CONFIRMED: L-HTC search, BOUNDS JAMES H → HOUSTON & TEXAS CENTRAL RAILWAY CO, DEED, D / 24, 03/22/1875, S MC ANULTY LEAGUE]
- **Additional L-HTC McAnulty findings**: (1) DR-0000D-00072, DEED, W H SMITH → H&TC RAILWAY CO, D / 72, 03/27/1875, S MC ANULTY SUR, 100 ACRES — second ROW conveyance 5 days later from adjacent landowner; (2) DR-00034-00256, RELEASE, H&TC RAILROAD CO → LINDLY J P, 34 / 256, 05/08/1901, S MC ANULTY LEAGUE, 103 ACRES — railroad released McAnulty League interests (pull PDF to determine nature of release).
- **Action needed**: Pull LC Vol. D Pg. 24.
operations.replace_with:
#### Deed [119] — J. H. Bounds → Houston & Texas Central Railway Co. (1871-05-03, Limestone) — **PDF CONFIRMED**
- **Transaction type**: Right-of-way conveyance — Sarah W. McNulty (McAnulty) League, 200-foot strip + blanket ROW
- **Citation**: LC Vol. D, Pg. 24, Inst# DR-0000D-00024 (RE-FILING — see recording history below); original recording: Book II, Pp. 798–799, Limestone County, recorded May 4, 1871
- **Property**: 200-foot-wide strip of land over the "Hall tract," a part of the Sarah W. McNulty League, Limestone County, on the waters of Tehuacana Creek; plus a blanket 200-foot ROW "over & upon any other lands now owned or which may hereafter be owned by me in this State, through which said Railroad has been or may be hereafter built." ⚠️ Survey name variant: "Sarah W. McNulty" — middle initial "W." and alternate surname spelling not found in any other instrument in the file set; same survey as "Sarah McAnulty" / "S. McAnulty" throughout.
- **Consideration**: $375 in coin ("Three Hundred & Seventy five coin Dollars") + enhanced value from railroad location and construction.
- **Upstream source — CRITICAL DISCOVERY**: The deed describes the Hall tract as "having been conveyed to me by T.J. Hall and wife by deed dated 15th Dec 1865." This **T.J. Hall → JH Bounds deed (Dec 15, 1865)** is a previously unidentified SECOND upstream McAnulty acquisition, **distinct from the Hare→Bounds source deed** identified by deed [123]. Dec 15, 1865 is now the **earliest documented date for any JH Bounds McAnulty League activity** — predating deed [123] (1869-08-03, Dunbar sale from the Hare tract) by over 3 years, and consistent with the family tree note that JH Bounds "migrated to Texas 1865." Recording citation for the Hall→Bounds deed NOT stated in this instrument. **Action: search LC grantee index under JH Bounds for Hall conveyance, Dec 1865.**
- **Additional grants**: Earth, material, timber, and stone from the described lands for railroad construction; full release from all damage claims for construction and ROW.
- **Mineral status**: No mineral reservation. Fee simple conveyance of the strip ("grant bargain sell & release... To have & to hold... forever"). Minerals under the 200-foot strip conveyed to H&TC Railway.
- **Parties**: Grantor: James H. Bounds ("of the County of Limestone & State of Texas"). Grantee: Houston & Texas Central Railway Company (granting clause); "Houston & Texas Central Railroad" (consideration clause) — corporate name variant within same instrument.
- **Witnesses**: L. P. Tutt, John A. Quinn.
- **Proving**: L. P. Tutt personally appeared before J. B. Vallandingham, Clerk of the District Court, Limestone County, May 3, 1871, and attested he saw JH Bounds sign at Bounds' request. ⚠️ No separate acknowledgment by Bounds himself; no privy examination of wife (Margaret E. Bounds did not join this deed). Whether absence of wife's joinder affects the ROW's validity depends on 1871 Texas homestead law — however, no downstream instrument questions the ROW, and the railroad was physically built on this corridor.
- **Recording history**: (1) Originally filed May 3, 1871 at 10:00 AM; recorded Book II, Pp. 798–799, Limestone County, May 4, 1871; J. B. Vallandingham, Clk D.C. & C.C.; Deputy: J. J. Gosbold. (2) **Certified copy re-filed** June 22, 1895 at 2:00 PM; recorded same day at 3:45 PM; S. D. Walker, Clk. This 1895 re-filing is the source of the Book D, Pg. 24 citation. ⚠️ **Index date 03/22/1875 is erroneous** — does not correspond to the execution date (1871-05-03), the original filing date (1871-05-03), or the re-filing date (1895-06-22). Probable cause: index confusion with the W. H. Smith companion ROW deed at D/72 (03/27/1875), which is also an H&TC McAnulty ROW conveyance in the same volume.
- **Significance**: (a) **Source deed for the H&TC Railroad ROW** running through the McAnulty League — the boundary feature referenced in deeds [22], [05]/[23], [116], and all downstream instruments. The ROW is the reason the Manning 3-acre Second Tract is stated as 2 acres net. (b) **Second upstream McAnulty source identified** — T.J. Hall → JH Bounds (Dec 15, 1865) is distinct from Thomas Hare → JH Bounds (identified by deed [123]); JH Bounds assembled his McAnulty holdings from multiple upstream sources. (c) **Earliest documented JH Bounds McAnulty date** — Dec 15, 1865 predates all other McAnulty instruments by 3+ years. (d) **Blanket ROW clause** — extends the 200-foot strip beyond the Hall tract to all JH Bounds lands, current and future; this authorized the ROW through the entire McAnulty holding including tracts that later became deed [22]. (e) Confirms the railroad was built or under construction by 1871 ("has built or may hereafter build").
- **Disposition status**: **DISPOSED — ROW CONVEYANCE OUT. NO REVERSION.** Full fee simple conveyance; no reversionary clause; land permanently conveyed to H&TC Railway.
- **Additional L-HTC McAnulty findings** (unchanged from index analysis): (1) DR-0000D-00072, DEED, W H SMITH → H&TC RAILWAY CO, D / 72, 03/27/1875, S MC ANULTY SUR, 100 ACRES — separate ROW conveyance from adjacent landowner ~4 years after deed [119] (NOT 5 days as previously stated based on erroneous index date); (2) DR-00034-00256, RELEASE, H&TC RAILROAD CO → LINDLY J P, 34 / 256, 05/08/1901, S MC ANULTY LEAGUE, 103 ACRES — railroad released McAnulty League interests 30 years after deed [119] (NOT 26 years as previously calculated). Pull PDF to determine nature of release.
- **Action completed**: PDF pulled; ISSUE-40 item [119] marked PULLED. ISSUE-34 updated (Hall→JH Bounds second upstream source).

operations.content: - **Deed [119] analysis (2026-03-31)**: PDF now pulled. Deed [119] (LC Vol. D, Pg. 24) is a **right-of-way conveyance dated May 3, 1871** — the index date of 03/22/1875 is erroneous (does not correspond to execution, original filing, or re-filing dates). Instrument conveys a 200-foot strip over the "Hall tract" on the Sarah W. McNulty (McAnulty) League from JH Bounds to Houston & Texas Central Railway Company for $375 coin, plus a blanket ROW over all other JH Bounds lands. Fee simple — no mineral reservation. **CRITICAL DISCOVERY — second upstream McAnulty source deed identified**: The deed describes the Hall tract as "having been conveyed to me by T.J. Hall and wife by deed dated 15th Dec 1865." This **T.J. Hall → JH Bounds** conveyance is distinct from the **Thomas Hare → JH Bounds** source identified by deed [123] — different grantor, different date. Dec 15, 1865 is now the **earliest documented date for any JH Bounds McAnulty League activity**, consistent with JH Bounds' 1865 migration to Texas. JH Bounds assembled his McAnulty holdings from at least two upstream sources (Hare and Hall) plus smaller acquisitions (Manning, Mallard, Longbotham, Smith). The Hall tract acreage is unknown — locating the Hall→Bounds deed (search LC grantee index, Dec 1865) will determine whether the Hare and Hall tracts together account for the "278 acre survey" or whether the 278-acre figure refers to the Hare tract alone. ⚠️ Survey name variant: "Sarah W. McNulty" (middle initial + alternate spelling) — unique to deed [119]; all other instruments use "McAnulty."

H&TC reconveyed McAnulty League interests 26 years after deed [119] (1875 ROW conveyance). Potentially significant for ISSUE-34 survey gap. Note: "Lindly J P" may or may not be J.P. Lindley of Holloway Survey chain (deeds [101]/[105]) — speculative; verify against source. MEDIUM-HIGH priority / ISSUE-34. [IDENTIFIED: L-HTC index, Session 8B, 2026-03-09]
